Okay, onboarding starts with written and verbal job offer. Okay, so now that you have gone through the interviewing process, the phone screening, the app practical application, checking their references, now you're like, okay, cool, I want to give a person a job, how do I present that job to them, right? Okay, so what do you include in a verbal offer? Because you need to do a verbal offer before you do a written. Okay, so one, you need to talk about how excited you are. So this is closing the sale, 101, all right? You are closing the sale, you need to be super excited, okay? So reinstate how excited you are, how you feel like they're a great fit for the company. You want to remind them of all the things that they said about like maybe it was your culture, the training, the experience they're gonna gain, whatever, of why they're gonna like the company, right? Then you're going to present to them the money, right? You're like, I'm giving you this offer for operations for a manager position at 40,000 a year, right? So you need to be very clear about how much money. Then you need to ask, you know, would you like to accept this job offer, right? And so from there, you want to get a response from them. And after you get that response, which is hopefully yes, you want to tell them what comes next. So what comes next? Make sure you tell them the next time you're communicating with them. So if that means, hey, awesome, I'm so excited, guess what Sally, like I'm gonna go ahead, I'm gonna get my computer right now, I'm gonna send you a written offer. It's gonna take me probably like an hour because I need to get together like a W9, a this, that, all that stuff. But I'm gonna send that over. And then once you fill out all that information, the job's locked in and it's yours. Okay, so be very clear about what comes next. Once you've confirmed and they're like, yeah, I'm so pumped, like I've been waiting for you to call me all this stuff, like so excited to be a part of the team. You're like, sweet, okay, I'm gonna go send you that written offer. And then you get off and you get on the computer and you create their written offer. Okay, and so I provide an example in this, the download section as well. And so it basically just highlights all of the things you wanna hit, like attaching a non-compete, making sure there are dog descriptions in there, making sure that you have their communication cycle in there, telling them who they report to, who their point of contact is, what their pay is, what their hours are, as well as it's at will employment, what is the first day of the job, and then what do they expect to come next. So a big thing is that you need to make it clear to them, they need to sign everything within 48 hours, and that then you will send them a follow-up email. Okay, so make sure you confirm what they need to do, as well as what you're going to do next.
